name: Miradouro da Senhora do Monte
descriptionText: Best panoramic view of Lisbon with São Jorge Castle and the Tagus River. Less crowded than other miradouros.
tips: Arrive 30 minutes before sunset. A 24-70mm lens is ideal. Summer light hits the castle perfectly.

name: Pink Street
descriptionText: Famous pink-painted street in Cais do Sodré. Great for colorful street photography and neon signs at night.
tips: Go before 8 AM to avoid crowds and capture clean compositions.

name: LX Factory Rooftop
descriptionText: Industrial-chic rooftop with views of the 25 de Abril Bridge. Great for portraits and urban shots.
tips: Access through the main LX Factory building elevator. Best light in late afternoon.

name: Trocadéro
descriptionText: Classic Eiffel Tower viewpoint. Iconic but crowded — worth it at the right time.
tips: Arrive before 6:30 AM for an almost empty Trocadéro. Use a telephoto lens to compress the tower.

name: Rue Crémieux
descriptionText: Colorful houses street perfect for portraits and vibrant travel shots.
tips: Be respectful — it's a residential street. Early morning = best light + fewer tourists.

name: Shibuya Sky
descriptionText: 360-degree rooftop observatory overlooking Shibuya Crossing and Tokyo skyline.
tips: Book tickets in advance for sunset slots. No tripods allowed on the rooftop.

name: Omoide Yokocho
descriptionText: Narrow alley of tiny yakitori stalls with glowing lanterns — classic Tokyo night photography.
tips: Bring a fast lens (f/1.8 or wider). Respect diners and don't block the narrow path.
